title: pluchea caspia
intro: Pluchea caspia, also known as the saltwort or sea lavender, is a perennial herb belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, digestive issues, and skin problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Pluchea caspia include its ability to reduce swelling, soothe digestive issues, and protect against microbial infections. Pluchea caspia is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
